instructions: Create `src/jitsu/core/storage.py` containing an `EpicStorage` class to encapsulate all JSON file I/O operations (loading epics, writing completed phases, managing the `completed_dir` archiving). Refactor the `run`, `auto`, and `submit` commands in `src/jitsu/cli/main.py` to use `EpicStorage` instead of bare `Path(...).read_text()` or `out.rename(...)`. Update `tests/cli/test_main.py` to patch this new storage layer.
anti_patterns: Do NOT leave any bare `open()` or `Path().read_text()` calls handling JSON files inside the CLI module.
Do NOT alter the actual schema or lifecycle of the Epic, only where the disk I/O occurs.
completion_criteria: `EpicStorage` fully encapsulates file I/O for epic tracking and archiving.

instructions: Create `src/jitsu/core/orchestrator.py` containing a `JitsuOrchestrator` class. Move the complex `_run_planner`, `_execute_phases`, and `_run_autonomous_loop` logic from `src/jitsu/cli/main.py` into this new class. The orchestrator should accept `JitsuPlanner` and `JitsuExecutor` via dependency injection. Clean up the boolean trap (`# noqa: FBT001, FBT002`) and import outside top-level (`# noqa: PLC0415`) violations that were inside these functions.
anti_patterns: Do NOT leave orchestration logic or LLM error handling loops inside `main.py`.
Do NOT carry over `# noqa` suppression flags into the new orchestrator module. Fix the underlying issues.
completion_criteria: `src/jitsu/core/orchestrator.py` contains all autonomous loop and execution logic.
The CLI `plan`, `run`, and `auto` commands strictly instantiate and call the `JitsuOrchestrator`.
Boolean trap and lazy import violations are eliminated.

instructions: First, move the raw TCP `_send_payload` logic from `cli/main.py` into a new `src/jitsu/server/client.py` file. Second, resolve the templates audit by creating `src/jitsu/templates/loader.py` with a `TemplateLoader` utility to abstract the `importlib.resources.files` boilerplate, and refactor the `init()` command in `cli/main.py` to use it. Finally, remove the blind exception catching (`# noqa: BLE001`) in the CLI `serve()` command, catching specific `OSError` or `anyio` exceptions instead.
anti_patterns: Do NOT leave `anyio` raw socket connection logic inside `cli/main.py`.
Do NOT leave any `# noqa: BLE001` markers in the CLI.
completion_criteria: Network client logic is encapsulated in `src/jitsu/server/client.py`.
The CLI layer is now purely a Typer routing layer with zero architectural violations.
